name: Safety Limitations review for Mitophagy Enhancers coverage repair bundle
summary: The bundle improves safety coverage by adding a renal-calculi case-series signal and by keeping frail older-adult safety results pending. It does not overuse short tolerability trials as chronic reassurance, and it clearly distinguishes diet-linked stone cases from unproven supplement causality.

findings.claim_or_issue: The support map treats short urolithin A trials as limited tolerability context and leaves chronic safety, urinary-stone risk, and frail-population safety unresolved.
findings.why_it_matters: Short trials are insufficient for chronic supplement risk-benefit judgments in older or frail adults.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the chronic safety and frail-population result gaps in the public outlook.

findings.claim_or_issue: The renal-calculi record includes the needed case-series, diet-exposure, and no-incidence caveats.
findings.why_it_matters: Those caveats prevent the safety signal from becoming an unsupported causal warning about supplements.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the caveats and watch for larger safety or pharmacovigilance evidence.
